_fixture_notes: Case 12: Recipe with every optional field omitted from the JSON entirely (key not present, not set to null). Includes only fields with 'Required: yes' in the spec table, plus the array fields that need to be empty-for-recipes (component_layers, derived_components) and meal_types/meal_formats which the spec treats as recipe-only. Spec gap: the template provides explicit null values for cuisine, dietary_category, macros, etc., and explicit [] for dietary_restrictions, nutritional_tags. Does the validator treat 'key missing' as equivalent to 'key present with null/empty value', or require all keys per the template? This fixture takes the aggressive 'omit if not required' interpretation. A 'minimal but template-conforming' companion fixture would test the other reading.
name: Boiled Egg

steps.title: Boil
steps.content: Lower {0001} into simmering water and cook for 7 minutes for a just-set yolk.
